instruction:
Input format: The model will receive one block of structured text that contains the following labeled fields:

1. Case title: Title of the security case on the SOAR platform
2. Case description: Description of the case (may include Markdown)
3. Task title: Title of the investigation task that contains the sub‑activity
4. Task description: Description of the investigation task
5. Activity title: Title of the sub‑activity you’re checking
6. Activity data: Narrative of the sub‑activity, analysis performed, SIEM queries that were run for it, and the event data that was returned

Your Goal: Inspect the Activity data and determine whether additional SIEM queries are necessary to complete the investigation
Decision Rule: If no additional SIEM queries are required to finish investigating the sub‑activity, deem it complete and reply with YES. Otherwise, if further SIEM queries would be needed, reply with NO.

Please explain your reasoning first in few sentenses. Then, in the next line, output either YES or NO.
If you answered NO, you must provide few sentence suggestions for future SIEM queries in your reasoning output.

If any of the queries failed to find any events, you must provide suggestions to generalize the query in your reasoning output in order to capture more events.

Example input (replace brackets with actual data):
Case title: [title]
Case description: [description]
Task title: [task title]
Task description: [task description]
Activity title: [activity title]
Activity data: [activity data]

Expected output:
Explanation: [Reasoning]
Final verdict: [YES / NO]
